reasoning: The question asks if Alexandra Kosteniuk will have a rating at least 1% higher than her March 30, 2025 rating (which is 2484) on each of the resolution dates. A 1% increase requires her to gain ~25 Elo points (reaching 2509). Kosteniuk, currently aged 40 and rated 2484, has a K-factor of 10. To gain 25 points in a single month requires her to score 2.5 points higher than her expected score, corresponding to a massive ~2700 Tournament Performance Rating (TPR) across ~10 games. Her recent early 2025 form in the Monaco Grand Prix saw her finish with roughly a 50% score (winning her final game), meaning her April rating will likely see only minor changes rather than a +25 point jump. Since the first two resolution dates are in April, the probability of her spontaneously surging to 2509+ by then is exceptionally low (below 5%). For the longer-term dates (June, September, and March 2026), she has more months to accumulate rating gains. Modeling Elo as a random walk with monthly standard deviations around 5 points, the odds of an underlying upward drift of +25 points reaches roughly 10-30% on a 6 to 12-month horizon, especially since her peak was much higher historically. Therefore, uncertainty increases with time, pushing distant-date probabilities closer to the uninformative prior.
